column1: # Crop moisture fractions used when rescaling dry-matter yields to fresh mass.
# - crop: crop identifier used in configuration files
# - moisture_fraction: share of fresh mass that is water (0-1 range)
# - food_conversion: how food_processing pathways translate dry-matter crop
#   bus mass into food bus mass.
#       food bus carries commercial commodity weight (storage moisture). This
#       matches FAOSTAT FBS supply units and the "as-purchased" basis used
#     - identity: skip the conversion. Used for crops whose GAEZ raster
#       already encodes the commercial product (not fresh weight):
#         * tea: GAEZ "yield" refers to dried tea-equivalent dry mass; the
#           (1-moisture) deflation handled at raster ingestion already
#           lands tea on a commercial basis (re-inflating would 4x oversupply).
#         * olive: GAEZ Module VI (RES06) OOC raster reports yield as
#           olive oil tons/ha (per v4 Table 9-2 convention
#           v5 for the OOC group). The crop bus therefore carries oil
#           tonnes directly; moisture is set to 0 (oil is anhydrous) and
#           the olive_oil pathway in foods.csv runs at 1:1.
#       commercial-weight moisture in this column (6.5% for cocoa
